tests.test_name: Espresso - PO - single test, core output structure
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a single Espresso test file receives structured JSON documentation with at least one named test and no missing or unresolved data.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The analysis produces visible output after submission

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or blanks
The output is correctly tagged as belonging to the Espresso test framework
At least one test case is detected and documented from the uploaded file
Each documented test case is identifiable by name

tests.test_name: Espresso - PO - Parameterized data-driven expands one test per row
tests.description: Verifies that a Product Owner uploading a data-driven Espresso test file sees the parameterized test expanded into one documented test entry per data row, resulting in three or more tests.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The analysis produces visible output after submission

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or blanks
The output is correctly tagged as belonging to the Espresso test framework
Data-driven tests are expanded so each parameter row becomes a separate documented test case
Each expanded test case variant is identifiable by name

tests.test_name: Espresso - QAM - single test, coverage matrix populated
tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a single Espresso test file receives documentation that includes a populated test coverage matrix alongside standard test name output.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The analysis produces visible output after submission

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or blanks
The output is correctly tagged as belonging to the Espresso test framework
At least one test case is detected and documented from the uploaded file
Each documented test case is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: The first test has a non-empty array 'test_coverage_matrix'
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The QA Manager role triggers generation of a coverage matrix, which is present and contains entries

tests.description: Verifies that a QA Manager uploading a data-driven Espresso test file sees the parameterized test collapsed into a single documented entry whose coverage matrix rows reflect each data example using the correct schema.

test_coverage.verification_step: Documentation appears in the output panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The analysis produces visible output after submission

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All fields in the output are fully populated with no placeholders or blanks
The output is correctly tagged as belonging to the Espresso test framework
Data-driven tests are collapsed into a single entry for the QA Manager role, rather than expanded
The collapsed test case is identifiable by name

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix row count matches the examples
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each data row from the parameterized test is represented as a separate row in the coverage matrix

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix rows use the correct schema for 'espresso'
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The structure of each coverage matrix row conforms to the expected Espresso-specific format
